sentences.english: My favorite season is summer.
There is no school.
sentences.korean: 학교도 없고 따뜻하고 해가 늦게 져요.

sentences.english: It's warm.
sentences.korean: 발코니에서 맥주 마시고 바비큐 할 수 있으니 정말 멋져요.

sentences.english: The sun sets late.
You can sit on your balcony and drink a beer and barbecue.
sentences.korean: / 발코니에서 맥주 마시고 바비큐 할 수 있다.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?

sentences.english: I just think it's fabulous.
My favorite season is winter, because there's a lot of snow and it's just beautiful.
When the countryside is covered with snow, it's perfectly white.
I love to build snowmen and have snowball fights with my friends.
My favorite season is springtime, because everything is getting all flowery and new and fresh again.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?

sentences.english: My least favorite is fall because you can't predict if it's going to be a warm day or a cold day.
sentences.korean: 모든 것이 꽃피고 새로워지니까요. 가장 싫은 건 가을인데 따뜻할지 추울지 예측할 수 없어서요.

sentences.english: My favorite season would be summer.
It's pretty cold in Canada, and it's nice to have a long, sunny, dry summer.
My least favorite is definitely winter.
I think it can be a little depressing.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?

sentences.english: My favorite season would probably be autumn.
I love the changing of the leaves, the crisp autumn wind that blows the leaves around.
sentences.korean: 나뭇잎이 변하는 것과 나뭇잎을 흩날리는 상쾌한 가을 바람을 좋아해요.

sentences.english: I love walking around to come home to a warm cup of hot chocolate.
sentences.korean: 걸어다니다 집에 와서 따뜻한 핫초코 마시는 걸 좋아해요.

sentences.english: In my country, we've only got two seasons — summer and winter.
sentences.korean: 우리나라는 여름과 겨울 두 계절만 있어요.

sentences.english: My favorite is summer, because of the sun and the warm weather, you can hang out with friends and do pretty much anything.
